author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Fixed the per-lane allocation hot path in ferray-fft/src/nd.rs::fft_along_axis.

Old code path:
- Each rayon worker called Vec::with_capacity(fft_len) per lane
- Pushed elements one at a time, then resize() to handle zero-padding
- Returned the per-lane Vec via map_init().collect()
- Result: num_lanes separate heap allocations + num_lanes Vec headers + an
  intermediate Vec<Vec<Complex<f64>>> wrapper, all just to feed the scatter
  step that copies them back into the final flat output

New code path:
- One contiguous lane_outputs buffer of num_lanes * fft_len Complex values
- par_chunks_mut(fft_len).zip(lane_starts.par_iter()).for_each_init(...)
- Each parallel iteration writes directly into its assigned chunk via
  indexed access (no Vec::push, no resize); the chunk's pre-zeroed
  positions naturally handle zero-padding when fft_len > axis_len
- FFT runs in-place on the chunk; scratch buffer is still per-thread via
  for_each_init
- A final sequential scatter step copies each chunk into the strided
  output positions — no allocation, just indexed memcpy

For an (M, N) array with FFT along axis 1, this drops M+1 allocations
to 2 (lane_outputs + final output). Per-iteration overhead drops from
'with_capacity + push loop + resize + collect' to plain slice writes.

3 new tests as regression guards: 4×8 multi-lane along axis 1 (delta
inputs verifying each lane's FFT), 8×4 multi-lane along axis 0 (the
strided-lane scatter case), and 3×2 multi-lane with zero-padding (3 input
lanes of length 2 padded to length 4 each). All tests verify the lane
results match hand-computed values.

Adjacent cleanup: 3 preexisting clippy errors (2 needless_range_loop in
new tests fixed pre-emptively, 1 unused IxDyn import in
ferray-fft/tests/property_tests.rs).

Result: 66 ferray-fft lib tests pass (was 63, +3), workspace tests +
ferray-fft clippy clean. Closes #433.
